# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-bqt [EPIC] · E7: Mixer Core   [● P0 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-28 · Updated: 2026-03-28

DESCRIPTION
Channel strip model: fader (logarithmic taper GainNode), pan (StereoPannerNode), mute, solo. Solo-in-place state machine with solo-isolate support (R-STA-04). Insert chain infrastructure (ordered array of effect slots, empty initially). Audio graph construction: per-track GainNode chain -> master bus GainNode -> hard limiter -> AudioContext.destination. CRITICAL SAFETY: Non-bypassable DynamicsCompressorNode as brickwall limiter (threshold=-1dBFS, ratio=20:1, knee=0, attack=0.001s) as final master bus node (INV-2). DFS cycle detection on every routing mutation (INV-1) -- insert-only is acyclic by construction but infrastructure must exist for E13. Clip playback: schedule AudioBufferSourceNode for clips in look-ahead window (reads ArrangementQuery). Canvas-based metering: peak + RMS computed via AnalyserNode, written to SharedArrayBuffer, read by Canvas at 60fps via rAF (NFR-13). Clip indicator with latching (R-UNW-01). Emergency mute on sustained clipping (>500ms). Mixer panel UI: vertical channel strips with fader, pan knob, mute/solo, meter, name. EARS: R-EVT-06, R-EVT-07, R-UBI-03, R-UBI-08, R-STA-04, R-UNW-01, R-UNW-05, NFR-01, NFR-02, NFR-09, NFR-13. VERTICAL SLICE MILESTONE: after this epic, users can import audio, arrange, and mix. QA: solo-in-place logic, metering accuracy, clip indicator behavior, cycle detection, hard limiter effectiveness.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-5he: E2: Neo-Brutalist Design System ● P1
  → ✓ long-running-harness-8ew: E3: Transport & Scheduling Engine ● P0

## THOROUGH EXPLORATION: BRUTALWAV DAW CODEBASE

Based on my comprehensive analysis of the codebase, here is the complete architecture and infrastructure of this Digital Audio Workstation:

---

## 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Root Configuration:**
- **package.json**: React 19.2.4, Zustand 5.0.12 (state), Zod 4.3.6 (validation), TypeScript 5.8
- **vite.config.ts**: Import aliases configured for @/, @audio/, @ui/, @state/
- **vitest.config.ts**: jsdom environment, test files in src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}
- **tsconfig.app.json**: Strict TypeScript (ES2022 target, React JSX, no unused vars/params)
- **HTML**: index.html with JetBrains Mono font preload, single <div id="root">

**Main Source Directories:**
```
/src/
├── audio/          (E1 - Audio engine: 27 files including scheduling, transport, media pool)
├── state/          (E6 - State management: Zustand store, session persistence, undo)
├── ui/             (E2 - UI components: 21 folders including primitives, controls, arrangement)
├── test/           (setup.ts with @testing-library/jest-dom)
├── App.tsx         (Root component bootstrapping)
├── main.tsx        (React entry point)
└── vite-env.d.ts
```

---

## 2. AUDIO ENGINE INFRASTRUCTURE (E1)

**Location:** `/src/audio/`

### Core AudioContext Management
- **engine-context.ts**: `createAudioEngine()` returns `AudioEngineContext` with:
  - Wraps native AudioContext (suspended on creation)
  - Methods: `resume()`, `close()`, `loadWorkletModule(url)`
  - State getter mapping: suspended/running/closed
  - **INV-1**: Cross-origin isolation required (headers in vite.config)

### Transport & Scheduling (E3)

**TransportClock** (`transport-clock.ts`):
- State machine: stopped/playing/paused
- Methods: play(), pause(), stop(), seek(sec), setBpm(bpm)
- Loop support: `setLoop()` with enabled/start/end
- SharedArrayBuffer writes for lock-free UI communication
- Sample-rate aware cursor tracking
- **INV-5**: Loop wrap detection via `updateCursor()` and `didLoopWrap()`

**TempoMap** (`tempo-map.ts`):
- Single BPM (currently 20-999 range)
- 4/4 time signature (hardcoded)
- MIDI standard: 480 ticks per beat
- Bar-Beat-Tick (BBT) conversions (1-indexed musician convention)
- Core: `secondsPerBeat()`, `secondsToBBT()`, `bbtToSeconds()`

**LookAheadScheduler** (`look-ahead-scheduler.ts`):
- Chris Wilson pattern: setInterval ~25ms polling
- Look-ahead window: ~100ms
- Calls `onTick(beatTime, beatNumber)` callback
- Handles loop boundary constraints
- Syncs beat phase on seek

**Metronome** (`metronome.ts`):
- Downbeat: 1000Hz (30ms), Upbeat: 800Hz (30ms)
- OscillatorNode bursts scheduled at exact AudioContext times
- **INV-6**: `silence()` and `dispose()` guarantee silence within 10ms via gain fade

**SharedArrayBuffer Layout** (`shared-buffer-layout.ts`, `message-protocol.ts`):
- `TransportLayout`: Cursor (Float64), State (Uint8), BPM (Float32)
- `MeteringLayout`: Master level and clip detection
- Zero-allocation pattern: SAB views created once

### Media Pool
**Location:** `/src/audio/media-pool/`

- **types.ts**: Defines `MediaPool`, `AudioSourceHandle`, `WaveformPeaks`, error types
- **media-pool-manager.ts**: Creates `createMediaPool(ctx, storage)` with:
  - `importFile(file)`: Decodes audio, stores in IDB, computes peaks
  - `getAudioBuffer(id)`: Cached buffer retrieval with lazy decode-on-demand
  - `getPeaks(id, samplesPerPeak)`: Waveform peak data for rendering
  - `removeSource(id)`: Cleanup (blob, metadata, peaks)
  - Count tracking, listSources()
  
- **decode-pipeline.ts**: Detects format (WAV/MP3/OGG/FLAC) via magic bytes, uses `ctx.decodeAudioData()`
- **idb-storage.ts**: IndexedDB with in-memory fallback
  - Tables: blobs (file data), metadata (AudioSourceHandle), peaks (WaveformPeaks)
- **waveform-peaks.ts**: Computes min/max interleaved pairs at configurable resolution

### Context & Providers
- **audio-engine-provider.tsx**: React Context provider for AudioEngineContext
- **audio-engine-ctx.ts**: React createContext() for AudioEngineCtx
- **use-audio-engine.ts**: Hook to consume AudioEngineContext
- **transport-provider.tsx**: TransportProvider wrapping DawShellInner
- **use-transport.ts**: 
  - `useTransportInit()`: Initializes clock, scheduler, metronome (used only by TransportProvider)
  - `useTransport()`: Consumer hook returning `UseTransportReturn` interface

---

## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT (Zustand Store)

**Location:** `/src/state/store.ts`

**DawStore Structure:**
```typescript
{
  // Transport (5 props)
  transportState: "stopped" | "playing" | "paused"
  bpm: number (120 default, 20-999 range)
  cursorSeconds: number
  loopEnabled: boolean
  loopStart/loopEnd: number

  // Engine (1 prop)
  engineStatus: "uninitialized" | "suspended" | "running" | "error"

  // Tracks & Clips (4 props)
  tracks: TrackModel[] (immutable)
  clips: Record<string, ClipModel> (by ID)
  selectedTrackIds: string[]
  selectedClipIds: string[]

  // Actions (30 methods)
  play/pause/stop/setBpm/setCursor/setLoop
  setEngineStatus
  addTrack/removeTrack/updateTrack/reorderTrack
  addClip/removeClip/moveClip/splitClip/trimClip/duplicateClip
  setSelectedTrackIds/setSelectedClipIds
  queryClipsAtTime(time): filters by mute/solo status
}
```

**Key Patterns:**
- Pure reducer functions with immutable array spreads
- Track clipIds stored separately (denormalized for fast lookup)
- Random UUIDs for clip IDs via `crypto.randomUUID()`
- Query method respects mute/solo state

### Track & Clip Models

**TrackModel** (`/src/state/track/types.ts`):
```typescript
{
  id: string
  name: string
  type: "audio" | "instrument"
  color: string (hex #rrggbb)
  muted: boolean
  solo: boolean
  armed: boolean
  volume: number (0-2)
  pan: number (-1 to 1)
  clipIds: readonly string[]
}
```

**ClipModel**:
```typescript
{
  id: string
  trackId: string
  sourceId: string (reference to MediaPool source)
  startTime: number (seconds on timeline)
  sourceOffset: number (seconds into source)
  duration: number (seconds)
  gain: number (0-2)
  fadeIn: number (seconds)
  fadeOut: number (seconds)
  name: string
}
```

**Schemas** (`track-schema.ts`): Zod validation for both models with constraints

### Track Commands (Undo Integration)

**Location:** `/src/state/track/track-commands.ts`

Implements 10 UndoCommand classes:
1. `AddTrackCommand` / `RemoveTrackCommand`: Track lifecycle
2. `UpdateTrackCommand`: Patch capture (old values) for undo
3. `ReorderTrackCommand`: Drag-reorder tracks
4. `AddClipCommand` / `RemoveClipCommand`: Clip lifecycle
5. `MoveClipCommand`: Clip repositioning with track transfer
6. `SplitClipCommand`: Split at time, captures rightClipId
7. `TrimClipCommand`: Adjust start/end, saves original
8. `DuplicateClipCommand`: Clone at position, tracks generatedId

Each command:
- Implements `execute()` and `undo()` 
- Captures state before mutation
- `serialize()` for potential persistence
- Handles edge cases (missing tracks, invalid clip splits)

### Session Persistence

**Location:** `/src/state/session/`

**session-schema.ts**: Zod schema with version control
```typescript
{
  version: 1
  meta: { name, createdAt, updatedAt }
  transport: { bpm, loopEnabled, loopStart, loopEnd }
  tracks: TrackSchema[]
  clips: ClipSchema[]
  mixer: { masterVolume }
}
```

**session-storage.ts**: Abstract storage interface
- `SessionStorage`: putSession, getSession, listSessions, deleteSession
- `createInMemorySessionStorage()`: Map-based fallback
- `createIndexedDBSessionStorage()`: IDB persistence with list/recover
- **session-recovery.ts**: Load most recent session, handle corruption

**save-queue.ts**: Batches writes (max 50 concurrent, 500ms debounce)
**auto-save.ts**: Background save every 30 seconds
**use-session-persistence.ts**: Hook hydrating store from storage, handles recovery

### Undo System

**Location:** `/src/state/undo/`

- **undo-command.ts**: Interface `{ type, execute(), undo(), serialize() }`
- **undo-manager.ts**: Stack-based system
  - `push(command)`: Clears redo on new commands
  - `undo()` / `redo()`: Move between stacks
  - Max 200 commands by default
  - Properties: `canUndo`, `canRedo`, `size`
- **undo-command-registry.ts**: Map-based command deserializer
- **shared-undo-manager.ts**: Singleton instance

---

## 4. UI COMPONENT HIERARCHY (E2 - Neo-Brutalist Design)

**Location:** `/src/ui/`

### Design Tokens (`tokens/tokens.ts`)
```typescript
{
  color: {
    black/white, blue/pink/green/amber/red,
    gray900/700/500/300/100
  }
  font: { mono: JetBrains Mono, sans: system-ui }
  space: { 1: 4px, 2: 8px, 3: 12px, 4: 16px, 6: 24px, 8: 32px, 12: 48px, 16: 64px }
  text: { xs: 10, sm: 12, base: 14, lg: 16, xl: 20, 2xl: 24, 3xl: 32, 4xl: 48 }
  border: { width: 2 }
}
```

### Primitives (`primitives/`)
- **Button.tsx**: variant (primary/secondary/ghost), size (sm/md/lg), disabled state
- **Toggle.tsx**: Checkbox variant with styled handle
- **Modal.tsx**: Centered overlay with backdrop
- **Select.tsx**: Dropdown with keyboard nav
- **ContextMenu.tsx**: Right-click menu
- **Tooltip.tsx**: Hover overlay

All use CSS Modules with neo-brutalist 2px borders, sharp corners, high contrast.

### Layout Components

**DawShell.tsx**: Main container (flex column, 100vh)
- Wraps entire UI in `<TransportProvider>`
- Sets global background-color: var(--color-black)
- Manages recovery dialog state
- Loads CommandRegistry, ShortcutMap, UndoManager

**Toolbar.tsx**: Brand + global controls

**TransportBar.tsx** (`transport/`):
- BPM input with bounds validation
- Play/Stop buttons (display Unicode: ▶ / ■)
- Cursor display (BBT format via SharedArrayBuffer)
- Loop toggle button
- Metronome toggle (MET)
- Uses `useTransport()` hook

**CursorDisplay.tsx**: Real-time BBT display reading SAB

### Arrangement Panel

**ArrangementPanel.tsx**: Canvas-based arrangement editor
- Full-screen canvas with ResizeObserver for DPR scaling
- Renders via `renderArrangement()` on RAF loop
- Wheel events: Ctrl+wheel zoom, Shift+wheel h-scroll, plain v-scroll
- Pointer events: drag/drop via `use-arrangement-interactions.ts`
- Grid snap support (1/4 beat default)

**arrangement-renderer.ts**: Pure canvas renderer
- Stages: background, ruler (bar labels), grid, track lanes, headers (color strip + name), clips, playhead
- Clip rendering: track color at 60% opacity, white border if selected
- Track header indicators: M (muted), S (solo) at right edge
- Playhead: red vertical line at cursor position
- All coordinates computed from view state (no DOM reading)

**hit-test.ts**: Point-in-clip detection, drag boundaries
**use-arrangement-interactions.ts**: Pointer drag/drop logic with snap

### Audio Controls (`controls/`)
- **Fader.tsx**: Vertical slider with keyboard support (arrows, PgUp/Dn, Home/End), drag with AbortController
- **RotaryKnob.tsx**: Rotational control
- **VuMeter.tsx**: Level meter display
- **StepButton.tsx**: Button for step sequencer
- **DrumPad.tsx**: Drum pad control

### Session UI (`session/`)
- **RecoveryDialog.tsx**: Accept/discard recovered sessions
- **useSessionShortcuts.ts**: Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+Y redo

### Keyboard System

**command-registry.ts**: Command bus
```typescript
register(command: { id, label, execute(), enabled?() })
execute(id) → bool
```

**shortcut-map.ts**: Binding resolver
```typescript
bind({ key, ctrl?, shift?, alt?, meta?, commandId })
resolve(KeyboardEvent) → commandId?
```

**useKeyboardShortcuts.ts**: Global listener, routes to CommandRegistry

### Media Pool UI (`media-pool/`)
- **MediaPoolPanel.tsx**: Import dialog, file list, waveform previews
- **WaveformPreview.tsx**: Renders peak data as canvas graph

---

## 5. TEST INFRASTRUCTURE

**Test Setup:**
- **vitest.config.ts**: jsdom environment, globals enabled
- **src/test/setup.ts**: @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest
- **Pattern**: *.test.{ts,tsx} colocated with source

**Mocking Strategy** (App.test.tsx):
- Mock AudioContext with state, sampleRate, currentTime
- Mock GainNode, OscillatorNode
- Mock audioWorklet.addModule()
- Stub global.AudioContext
- Test cross-origin isolation check

**Coverage**: 
- Excluded: test/, vite-env.d.ts, main.tsx
- Provider: v8
- Most files have companion .test files

---

## 6. IMPORT ALIAS PATTERNS

Configured in **vite.config.ts** and **tsconfig.app.json**:
```
@/      → src/
@audio/ → src/audio/
@ui/    → src/ui/
@state/ → src/state/
```

**Usage Pattern:**
```typescript
import { useDawStore } from "@state/index";
import { renderArrangement } from "@ui/arrangement";
import { createAudioEngine } from "@audio/index";
```

---

## 7. KEY ARCHITECTURAL INVARIANTS

From code comments and design:

| INV # | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **INV-1** | Cross-origin isolation required (COOP/COEP headers) |
| **INV-3** | Zero-allocation: SAB views created once per context |
| **INV-5** | Loop wrap detection: `updateCursor()` + `didLoopWrap()` |
| **INV-6** | Metronome silence within 10ms (gain fade + disconnect) |

---

## 8. RUNTIME FLOW

1. **App.tsx**: Check crossOriginIsolated, show ClickToStart
2. **User click**: `createAudioEngine()` → `AudioContext()` suspended
3. **resume()**: Promisified, updates store to "running"
4. **MediaPool init**: Load existing sources from IDB
5. **DawShell**: Mount TransportProvider
6. **useTransportInit()**: Create Clock, Scheduler, Metronome
7. **ArrangementPanel**: Render on RAF, respond to pointer events
8. **Playback**: Scheduler fires every 25ms, schedules beats via metronome
9. **Session**: Auto-save every 30s, recovery on startup

---

## 9. KEY FILES BY RESPONSIBILITY

| Responsibility | Key Files |
|---|---|
| **Audio Context** | engine-context.ts, use-audio-engine.ts |
| **Transport/Scheduling** | transport-clock.ts, look-ahead-scheduler.ts, use-transport.ts |
| **Playback** | metronome.ts, tempo-map.ts |
| **State** | store.ts, track-commands.ts |
| **Persistence** | session-storage.ts, auto-save.ts, use-session-persistence.ts |
| **Undo** | undo-manager.ts, undo-command.ts, track-commands.ts |
| **Arrangement UI** | arrangement-renderer.ts, ArrangementPanel.tsx, hit-test.ts |
| **Transport UI** | TransportBar.tsx, CursorDisplay.tsx, useTransportShortcuts.ts |
| **Keyboard** | command-registry.ts, shortcut-map.ts, useKeyboardShortcuts.ts |
| **Media Pool** | media-pool-manager.ts, idb-storage.ts, decode-pipeline.ts |
| **Design System** | tokens.ts, primitives/*, Button.module.css, etc. |

---

## 10. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY

**Core:**
- React 19.2.4 + React DOM
- Zustand 5.0.12 (state)
- Zod 4.3.6 (validation)

**Build:**
- Vite 8.0.2 + React plugin
- TypeScript 5.8.3 (strict)
- ESLint + Prettier + Husky

**Testing:**
- Vitest 4.1.1
- @testing-library/react + jest-dom
- jsdom environment

**No external audio libraries** — all Web Audio API native.

---

This is a well-architected, type-safe DAW with clear separation of concerns: audio layer (E1), state (E3/E6), and UI (E2) with a neo-brutalist design system.

6. `SplitClipCommand`: Split at time, captures rightClipId
7. `TrimClipCommand`: Adjust start/end, saves original
8. `DuplicateClipCommand`: Clone at position, tracks generatedId

Each command:
- Implements `execute()` and `undo()` 
- Captures state before mutation
- `serialize()` for potential persistence
- Handles edge cases (missing tracks, invalid clip splits)

### Session Persistence

**Location:** `/src/state/session/`

**session-schema.ts**: Zod schema with version control
```typescript
{
  version: 1
  meta: { name, createdAt, updatedAt }
  transport: { bpm, loopEnabled, loopStart, loopEnd }
  tracks: TrackSchema[]
  clips: ClipSchema[]
  mixer: { masterVolume }
}
```

**session-storage.ts**: Abstract storage interface
- `SessionStorage`: putSession, getSession, listSessions, deleteSession
- `createInMemorySessionStorage()`: Map-based fallback
- `createIndexedDBSessionStorage()`: IDB persistence with list/recover
- **session-recovery.ts**: Load most recent session, handle corruption

**save-queue.ts**: Batches writes (max 50 concurrent, 500ms debounce)
**auto-save.ts**: Background save every 30 seconds
**use-session-persistence.ts**: Hook hydrating store from storage, handles recovery

### Undo System

**Location:** `/src/state/undo/`

- **undo-command.ts**: Interface `{ type, execute(), undo(), serialize() }`
- **undo-manager.ts**: Stack-based system
  - `push(command)`: Clears redo on new commands
  - `undo()` / `redo()`: Move between stacks
  - Max 200 commands by default
  - Properties: `canUndo`, `canRedo`, `size`
- **undo-command-registry.ts**: Map-based command deserializer
- **shared-undo-manager.ts**: Singleton instance

---

## 4. UI COMPONENT HIERARCHY (E2 - Neo-Brutalist Design)

**Location:** `/src/ui/`

### Design Tokens (`tokens/tokens.ts`)
```typescript
{
  color: {
    black/white, blue/pink/green/amber/red,
    gray900/700/500/300/100
  }
  font: { mono: JetBrains Mono, sans: system-ui }
  space: { 1: 4px, 2: 8px, 3: 12px, 4: 16px, 6: 24px, 8: 32px, 12: 48px, 16: 64px }
  text: { xs: 10, sm: 12, base: 14, lg: 16, xl: 20, 2xl: 24, 3xl: 32, 4xl: 48 }
  border: { width: 2 }
}
```

### Primitives (`primitives/`)
- **Button.tsx**: variant (primary/secondary/ghost), size (sm/md/lg), disabled state
- **Toggle.tsx**: Checkbox variant with styled handle
- **Modal.tsx**: Centered overlay with backdrop
- **Select.tsx**: Dropdown with keyboard nav
- **ContextMenu.tsx**: Right-click menu
- **Tooltip.tsx**: Hover overlay

All use CSS Modules with neo-brutalist 2px borders, sharp corners, high contrast.

### Layout Components

**DawShell.tsx**: Main container (flex column, 100vh)
- Wraps entire UI in `<TransportProvider>`
- Sets global background-color: var(--color-black)
- Manages recovery dialog state
- Loads CommandRegistry, ShortcutMap, UndoManager

**Toolbar.tsx**: Brand + global controls

**TransportBar.tsx** (`transport/`):
- BPM input with bounds validation
- Play/Stop buttons (display Unicode: ▶ / ■)
- Cursor display (BBT format via SharedArrayBuffer)
- Loop toggle button
- Metronome toggle (MET)
- Uses `useTransport()` hook

**CursorDisplay.tsx**: Real-time BBT display reading SAB

### Arrangement Panel

**ArrangementPanel.tsx**: Canvas-based arrangement editor
- Full-screen canvas with ResizeObserver for DPR scaling
- Renders via `renderArrangement()` on RAF loop
- Wheel events: Ctrl+wheel zoom, Shift+wheel h-scroll, plain v-scroll
- Pointer events: drag/drop via `use-arrangement-interactions.ts`
- Grid snap support (1/4 beat default)

**arrangement-renderer.ts**: Pure canvas renderer
- Stages: background, ruler (bar labels), grid, track lanes, headers (color strip + name), clips, playhead
- Clip rendering: track color at 60% opacity, white border if selected
- Track header indicators: M (muted), S (solo) at right edge
- Playhead: red vertical line at cursor position
- All coordinates computed from view state (no DOM reading)

**hit-test.ts**: Point-in-clip detection, drag boundaries
**use-arrangement-interactions.ts**: Pointer drag/drop logic with snap

### Audio Controls (`controls/`)
- **Fader.tsx**: Vertical slider with keyboard support (arrows, PgUp/Dn, Home/End), drag with AbortController
- **RotaryKnob.tsx**: Rotational control
- **VuMeter.tsx**: Level meter display
- **StepButton.tsx**: Button for step sequencer
- **DrumPad.tsx**: Drum pad control

### Session UI (`session/`)
- **RecoveryDialog.tsx**: Accept/discard recovered sessions
- **useSessionShortcuts.ts**: Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+Y redo

### Keyboard System

**command-registry.ts**: Command bus
```typescript
register(command: { id, label, execute(), enabled?() })
execute(id) → bool
```

**shortcut-map.ts**: Binding resolver
```typescript
bind({ key, ctrl?, shift?, alt?, meta?, commandId })
resolve(KeyboardEvent) → commandId?
```

**useKeyboardShortcuts.ts**: Global listener, routes to CommandRegistry

### Media Pool UI (`media-pool/`)
- **MediaPoolPanel.tsx**: Import dialog, file list, waveform previews
- **WaveformPreview.tsx**: Renders peak data as canvas graph

---

## 5. TEST INFRASTRUCTURE

**Test Setup:**
- **vitest.config.ts**: jsdom environment, globals enabled
- **src/test/setup.ts**: @testing-library/jest-dom/vitest
- **Pattern**: *.test.{ts,tsx} colocated with source

**Mocking Strategy** (App.test.tsx):
- Mock AudioContext with state, sampleRate, currentTime
- Mock GainNode, OscillatorNode
- Mock audioWorklet.addModule()
- Stub global.AudioContext
- Test cross-origin isolation check

**Coverage**: 
- Excluded: test/, vite-env.d.ts, main.tsx
- Provider: v8
- Most files have companion .test files

---

## 6. IMPORT ALIAS PATTERNS

Configured in **vite.config.ts** and **tsconfig.app.json**:
```
@/      → src/
@audio/ → src/audio/
@ui/    → src/ui/
@state/ → src/state/
```

**Usage Pattern:**
```typescript
import { useDawStore } from "@state/index";
import { renderArrangement } from "@ui/arrangement";
import { createAudioEngine } from "@audio/index";
```

---

## 7. KEY ARCHITECTURAL INVARIANTS

From code comments and design:

| INV # | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **INV-1** | Cross-origin isolation required (COOP/COEP headers) |
| **INV-3** | Zero-allocation: SAB views created once per context |
| **INV-5** | Loop wrap detection: `updateCursor()` + `didLoopWrap()` |
| **INV-6** | Metronome silence within 10ms (gain fade + disconnect) |

---

## 8. RUNTIME FLOW

1. **App.tsx**: Check crossOriginIsolated, show ClickToStart
2. **User click**: `createAudioEngine()` → `AudioContext()` suspended
3. **resume()**: Promisified, updates store to "running"
4. **MediaPool init**: Load existing sources from IDB
5. **DawShell**: Mount TransportProvider
6. **useTransportInit()**: Create Clock, Scheduler, Metronome
7. **ArrangementPanel**: Render on RAF, respond to pointer events
8. **Playback**: Scheduler fires every 25ms, schedules beats via metronome
9. **Session**: Auto-save every 30s, recovery on startup

---

## 9. KEY FILES BY RESPONSIBILITY

| Responsibility | Key Files |
|---|---|
| **Audio Context** | engine-context.ts, use-audio-engine.ts |
| **Transport/Scheduling** | transport-clock.ts, look-ahead-scheduler.ts, use-transport.ts |
| **Playback** | metronome.ts, tempo-map.ts |
| **State** | store.ts, track-commands.ts |
| **Persistence** | session-storage.ts, auto-save.ts, use-session-persistence.ts |
| **Undo** | undo-manager.ts, undo-command.ts, track-commands.ts |
| **Arrangement UI** | arrangement-renderer.ts, ArrangementPanel.tsx, hit-test.ts |
| **Transport UI** | TransportBar.tsx, CursorDisplay.tsx, useTransportShortcuts.ts |
| **Keyboard** | command-registry.ts, shortcut-map.ts, useKeyboardShortcuts.ts |
| **Media Pool** | media-pool-manager.ts, idb-storage.ts, decode-pipeline.ts |
| **Design System** | tokens.ts, primitives/*, Button.module.css, etc. |

---

## 10. DEPENDENCIES SUMMARY

**Core:**
- React 19.2.4 + React DOM
- Zustand 5.0.12 (state)
- Zod 4.3.6 (validation)

**Build:**
- Vite 8.0.2 + React plugin
- TypeScript 5.8.3 (strict)
- ESLint + Prettier + Husky

**Testing:**
- Vitest 4.1.1
- @testing-library/react + jest-dom
- jsdom environment

**No external audio libraries** — all Web Audio API native.

---

This is a well-architected, type-safe DAW with clear separation of concerns: audio layer (E1), state (E3/E6), and UI (E2) with a neo-brutalist design system.

1	# BRUTALWAV: Browser-Based Digital Audio Workstation
2	
3	## System Overview
4	
5	BRUTALWAV is a professional browser-based Digital Audio Workstation built on the Web Audio API. It provides multi-track audio and MIDI arrangement, a full-featured synthesizer, TR-808 drum machine (sample-based with tweakable parameters), effects processing, full mixer routing, modulation matrix, arpeggiator, and a piano roll editor -- all within a Neo-Brutalist interface that exposes its structural mechanics.
6	
7	**Target users**: Musicians, producers, and sound designers seeking a capable, browser-native production environment.
8	
9	**Tech stack**: React 19 + TypeScript (strict), Vite, Zustand (UI state), Web Audio API with native nodes for standard effects + AudioWorklet for custom DSP. Canvas/direct DOM for high-frequency rendering (metering, waveforms, piano roll).
10	
11	**Design direction**: Neo-Brutalist Expressionism with bold color accents. JetBrains Mono for data/values, system neo-grotesque for headings. 8px spatial grid. High-contrast black/white base with electric blue, hot pink, acid green, amber, and red accent palette.
12	
13	**Advisory profile**: `webapp` (data-dense, state-heavy, real-time interactive)
14	
15	**Build-great-things note**: All user-facing epics should invoke `/compound:build-great-things` during their work phase. This DAW requires excellence across all six build phases: Foundation (IA, state architecture), Structure (grid, responsive, component architecture), Craft (typography, color system, visual hierarchy), Motion & Interaction (micro-interactions, drag-and-drop, keyboard shortcuts), Performance & Polish (loading states, edge states, accessibility), and Launch (onboarding).
16	
17	---
18	
19	## EARS Requirements
20	
21	### R-UBI: Ubiquitous Requirements (always true)
22	
23	| ID | Requirement |
24	|----|-------------|
25	| R-UBI-01 | The system shall render all audio through the Web Audio API AudioContext |
26	| R-UBI-02 | The system shall use native Web Audio nodes (BiquadFilterNode, DynamicsCompressorNode, ConvolverNode, GainNode, etc.) for standard effects processing, and AudioWorklet processors for custom DSP (synth voices, custom envelopes, modulation routing). No ScriptProcessorNode. |
27	| R-UBI-03 | The system shall maintain separate audio-thread state and UI-thread state, communicating via lock-free mechanisms (MessagePort, SharedArrayBuffer) |
28	| R-UBI-04 | The system shall use sample-accurate scheduling via AudioContext.currentTime for all timed audio events |
29	| R-UBI-05 | The system shall preserve non-destructive editing: clips reference source audio without modifying originals |
30	| R-UBI-06 | The system shall apply the 8px spatial grid for all UI layout |

# BRUTALWAV: Browser-Based Digital Audio Workstation

## System Overview

BRUTALWAV is a professional browser-based Digital Audio Workstation built on the Web Audio API. It provides multi-track audio and MIDI arrangement, a full-featured synthesizer, TR-808 drum machine (sample-based with tweakable parameters), effects processing, full mixer routing, modulation matrix, arpeggiator, and a piano roll editor -- all within a Neo-Brutalist interface that exposes its structural mechanics.

**Target users**: Musicians, producers, and sound designers seeking a capable, browser-native production environment.

**Tech stack**: React 19 + TypeScript (strict), Vite, Zustand (UI state), Web Audio API with native nodes for standard effects + AudioWorklet for custom DSP. Canvas/direct DOM for high-frequency rendering (metering, waveforms, piano roll).

**Design direction**: Neo-Brutalist Expressionism with bold color accents. JetBrains Mono for data/values, system neo-grotesque for headings. 8px spatial grid. High-contrast black/white base with electric blue, hot pink, acid green, amber, and red accent palette.

**Advisory profile**: `webapp` (data-dense, state-heavy, real-time interactive)

**Build-great-things note**: All user-facing epics should invoke `/compound:build-great-things` during their work phase. This DAW requires excellence across all six build phases: Foundation (IA, state architecture), Structure (grid, responsive, component architecture), Craft (typography, color system, visual hierarchy), Motion & Interaction (micro-interactions, drag-and-drop, keyboard shortcuts), Performance & Polish (loading states, edge states, accessibility), and Launch (onboarding).

---

## EARS Requirements

### R-UBI: Ubiquitous Requirements (always true)

| ID | Requirement |
|----|-------------|
| R-UBI-01 | The system shall render all audio through the Web Audio API AudioContext |
| R-UBI-02 | The system shall use native Web Audio nodes (BiquadFilterNode, DynamicsCompressorNode, ConvolverNode, GainNode, etc.) for standard effects processing, and AudioWorklet processors for custom DSP (synth voices, custom envelopes, modulation routing). No ScriptProcessorNode. |
| R-UBI-03 | The system shall maintain separate audio-thread state and UI-thread state, communicating via lock-free mechanisms (MessagePort, SharedArrayBuffer) |
| R-UBI-04 | The system shall use sample-accurate scheduling via AudioContext.currentTime for all timed audio events |
| R-UBI-05 | The system shall preserve non-destructive editing: clips reference source audio without modifying originals |
| R-UBI-06 | The system shall apply the 8px spatial grid for all UI layout |
| R-UBI-07 | The system shall support undo/redo for all user-initiated edit operations via command pattern |
| R-UBI-08 | The system shall display real-time audio metering (peak + RMS) for all active tracks and master |
| R-UBI-09 | The system shall follow the Neo-Brutalist design system: JetBrains Mono for data, high-contrast palette, visible structural borders, exposed grid |
| R-UBI-10 | The system shall enforce TypeScript strict mode with noUncheckedIndexedAccess |
| R-UBI-11 | The system shall maintain all editable session state as a serializable JSON structure |

### R-EVT: Event-Driven Requirements

| ID | Trigger | Requirement |
|----|---------|-------------|
| R-EVT-01 | When the user presses Play | The system shall begin playback from the current cursor position, scheduling audio events using the look-ahead scheduler pattern |
| R-EVT-02 | When the user presses Stop | The system shall stop all audio playback, silence all outputs, and return the cursor to the last play-start position |
| R-EVT-03 | When the user presses Pause | The system shall suspend playback at the current position, retaining cursor position for resume |
| R-EVT-04 | When the user triggers a MIDI note (keyboard/mouse on piano roll or virtual keyboard) | The system shall allocate a synth voice, apply the current patch parameters (oscillators, filter, envelopes, LFOs), and route through the instrument track's signal chain within 10ms |
| R-EVT-05 | When the user imports an audio file (WAV/MP3/OGG/FLAC) | The system shall decode the file via AudioContext.decodeAudioData, store it in the media pool, and create a clip on the selected track |
| R-EVT-06 | When the user creates a new track | The system shall add a channel strip to the mixer (fader, pan, mute, solo, inserts, sends) and a lane in the arrangement view |
| R-EVT-07 | When the user adds an effect to a track insert slot | The system shall instantiate the corresponding AudioNode(s) and insert them into the track's audio graph chain |
| R-EVT-08 | When the user changes a synth parameter (oscillator type, filter cutoff, envelope, LFO rate, etc.) | The system shall apply the change in real-time via AudioParam scheduling with no audible glitch |
| R-EVT-09 | When the user clicks on the arrangement timeline | The system shall move the playback cursor to the clicked position, snapped to the current grid resolution |
| R-EVT-10 | When the user drags a clip on the arrangement | The system shall move the clip non-destructively, updating its timeline position and displaying ghost preview during drag |
| R-EVT-11 | When the user splits a clip | The system shall create two clips from the original, each referencing the same source audio with adjusted offsets |
| R-EVT-12 | When the user triggers a step on the 808 sequencer | The system shall play the corresponding drum sample (bass drum, snare, hi-hat, etc.) with user-adjustable parameters (tone, decay, tune, volume) via playback rate, amplitude envelope, and filter manipulation |
| R-EVT-13 | When the user saves the session | The system shall serialize the full session state to JSON and persist it to browser storage (IndexedDB) |
| R-EVT-14 | When the user initiates bounce/export | The system shall render the session to an audio buffer via OfflineAudioContext and offer download as WAV |
| R-EVT-15 | When the user activates the arpeggiator | The system shall auto-play held notes in the selected pattern (up/down/up-down/random) at the tempo-synced rate |
| R-EVT-16 | When the user adjusts tempo (BPM) | The system shall update the transport clock and reschedule all tempo-dependent events (metronome, arpeggiator, synced LFOs, step sequencer) |
| R-EVT-17 | When the user presses a keyboard shortcut | The system shall execute the mapped command without requiring mouse interaction |

### R-STA: State-Driven Requirements

| ID | State | Requirement |
|----|-------|-------------|
| R-STA-01 | While the transport is playing | The system shall advance the playback cursor, schedule audio events via the look-ahead scheduler, and update metering at 60fps |
| R-STA-02 | While a track is armed for recording | The system shall display the track in recording-ready state (hot pink accent) and monitor audio input if applicable |
| R-STA-03 | While loop mode is active | The system shall return the playback cursor to the loop start point when it reaches the loop end point, with seamless crossfade |
| R-STA-04 | While a track is soloed | The system shall mute all non-soloed tracks (solo-in-place behavior), except tracks with solo-isolate enabled |
| R-STA-05 | While the synthesizer is in legato mode | The system shall not re-trigger envelopes for overlapping notes, instead gliding pitch between notes |
| R-STA-06 | While the 808 sequencer is running | The system shall advance through the 16-step pattern at the current tempo, triggering drum sounds with accent and flam as programmed |
| R-STA-07 | While the arpeggiator latch is on | The system shall continue playing the arpeggio pattern even after all keys are released |
| R-STA-08 | While AudioContext is suspended (no user gesture yet) | The system shall display a clear prompt to click/interact to start audio, and resume the context on the first user gesture |
| R-STA-09 | While any parameter automation is active | The system shall apply automated parameter changes at sample-accurate timing, overriding manual knob positions |

### R-UNW: Unwanted Behavior Requirements

| ID | Condition | Requirement |
|----|-----------|-------------|
| R-UNW-01 | If audio output clips (exceeds 0dBFS) | The system shall activate the clip indicator on the affected channel and master, persisting until manually cleared |
| R-UNW-02 | If AudioWorklet processing exceeds the quantum budget (128 samples) | The system shall log the overrun event and continue processing without crashing |
| R-UNW-03 | If audio file decode fails | The system shall display an error with the file name and format, suggesting supported alternatives |
| R-UNW-04 | If browser tab loses focus during playback | The system shall continue audio playback uninterrupted (no throttling of audio thread) |
| R-UNW-05 | If the user attempts to create a feedback loop in routing | The system shall perform DFS cycle detection on the signal graph before applying any routing mutation, reject the command, and display a warning. This is a mandatory pre-condition for every addRoute, addSend, and addInsert operation. |
| R-UNW-06 | If IndexedDB storage is full during session save | The system shall notify the user and offer to export the session as a downloadable file |
| R-UNW-07 | If a synthesizer voice exceeds polyphony limit | The system shall steal the oldest or lowest-priority voice using the configured voice allocation strategy |
| R-UNW-08 | If the session JSON is malformed on load | The system shall display a recovery dialog and attempt to load as much of the session as possible |

### R-OPT: Optional/Feature Requirements

| ID | Feature | Requirement |
|----|---------|-------------|
| R-OPT-01 | Where MIDI input device is connected | The system shall accept MIDI note and CC messages, routing them to the selected instrument track |
| R-OPT-02 | Where the user enables sidechain compression | The system shall route the sidechain source signal to the compressor's detector input on the target track |
| R-OPT-03 | Where the user activates dark mode / light mode toggle | The system shall switch the full UI to the alternate Neo-Brutalist theme variant |
| R-OPT-04 | Where the user opens the modulation matrix | The system shall display all available modulation sources and destinations with drag-to-connect routing |
| R-OPT-05 | Where the user loads a preset | The system shall deserialize the preset JSON and apply all synthesizer/effect parameters atomically |

participant Master
    participant Speakers

    User->>UI: Press Play
    UI->>Transport: play()
    Transport->>Scheduler: start look-ahead loop
    loop Every 25ms (look-ahead)
        Scheduler->>Scheduler: Check events in next 100ms window
        Scheduler->>AudioGraph: Schedule source.start(time), noteOn(time)
    end
    AudioGraph->>Mixer: Per-track audio signal
    Mixer->>Mixer: Apply inserts, fader, pan, sends
    Mixer->>Master: Sum to master bus
    Master->>Speakers: AudioContext.destination
    Transport->>UI: Cursor position update (rAF)
```

### 808 Step Sequencer Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Sequencer
    participant Transport
    participant Scheduler
    participant DrumVoice
    participant Mixer

    Transport->>Sequencer: Beat clock tick
    Sequencer->>Sequencer: Advance to next step (0-15)
    Sequencer->>Scheduler: Read pattern: which drums active on this step
    loop For each active drum on step
        Scheduler->>DrumVoice: triggerDrum(type, accent, time)
        DrumVoice->>DrumVoice: Synthesize (oscillator + noise + filter + envelope)
        DrumVoice->>Mixer: Audio output to drum bus
    end
```

1	/**
2	 * SharedArrayBuffer byte layout for lock-free audio-thread -> UI-thread communication.
3	 * All offsets are in bytes. Float32 values use 4-byte alignment.
4	 *
5	 * INV-3: Zero-allocation convention -- all buffers pre-allocated at init,
6	 * no object/array creation in the render loop.
7	 */
8	
9	/** Byte offsets into the shared metering buffer */
10	export const MeteringLayout = {
11	  /** Master peak L (Float32) */
12	  MASTER_PEAK_L: 0,
13	  /** Master peak R (Float32) */
14	  MASTER_PEAK_R: 4,
15	  /** Master RMS L (Float32) */
16	  MASTER_RMS_L: 8,
17	  /** Master RMS R (Float32) */
18	  MASTER_RMS_R: 12,
19	  /** Per-track metering starts here. Each track: peakL, peakR, rmsL, rmsR (16 bytes) */
20	  TRACKS_OFFSET: 16,
21	  /** Bytes per track metering block */
22	  BYTES_PER_TRACK: 16,
23	} as const;
24	
25	/**
26	 * Byte offsets into the shared transport/cursor buffer.
27	 *
28	 * Note on atomics: Float32/Float64 views cannot use Atomics directly.
29	 * CURSOR_SECONDS and BPM must be read/written via integer-typed views
30	 * (e.g., Int32Array for BPM, BigInt64Array for CURSOR_SECONDS) or
31	 * accept non-atomic access with appropriate fencing.
32	 */
33	export const TransportLayout = {
34	  /** Playback cursor position in seconds (Float64, 8 bytes) */
35	  CURSOR_SECONDS: 0,
36	  /** Transport state: 0=stopped, 1=playing, 2=paused (Uint8) */
37	  STATE: 8,
38	  // Bytes 9-11: alignment padding (reserved, do not use)
39	  /** Current BPM (Float32, 4-byte aligned) */
40	  BPM: 12,
41	  /** Total size of the transport buffer */
42	  TOTAL_BYTES: 16,
43	} as const;
44	
45	export type SharedArrayBufferLayout = {
46	  metering: SharedArrayBuffer;
47	  transport: SharedArrayBuffer;
48	};
49	
50	/**
51	 * Creates the shared buffers for audio-thread <-> UI-thread communication.
52	 * @param maxTracks - Maximum number of tracks supported (default 64)
53	 */
54	export function createSharedBuffers(maxTracks = 64): SharedArrayBufferLayout {
55	  const meteringSize =
56	    MeteringLayout.TRACKS_OFFSET + maxTracks * MeteringLayout.BYTES_PER_TRACK;
57	  return {
58	    metering: new SharedArrayBuffer(meteringSize),
59	    transport: new SharedArrayBuffer(TransportLayout.TOTAL_BYTES),
60	  };
61	}
